name: DUCOMMUN INC /DE/
business: Ducommun designs and manufactures high-performance electronic and structural products for high-cost-of-failure applications, primarily in the aerospace and defense markets, and also serves industrial, medical, and other industries. The company operates through two segments: Electronic Systems (high-reliability electronic and electromechanical products) and Structural Systems (complex aerostructure components and assemblies).
sellsTo: original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) in aerospace and defense
U.S. Government and its prime contractors

exposures.sentence: Our largest end-use markets are the aerospace and defense markets, and our revenues from these markets represented 96% of our total net revenues in 2025.
Revenues from the commercial aerospace end-use market represented 38% of our total net revenues for 2025.
Revenues from the military and space end-use market in 2025 represented 58% of our total net revenues during 2025.
Revenues from the military and space end-use market in 2025 represented 58% of our total net revenues during 2025.
Our products are used in heavy industrial manufacturing systems and certain medical applications.
Our products are used in heavy industrial manufacturing systems and certain medical applications.

catalysts.event: FAA cleared Boeing's plan to raise 737 MAX production from 38 to 42 airplanes per month
catalysts.sentence: More recently, Boeing announced that the FAA cleared Boeing's plan to raise 737 MAX production from 38 airplanes to 42 airplanes per month.

catalysts.event: President Trump signed funding package ending U.S. government shutdown, ensuring full-year funding through September 2026
catalysts.sentence: On February 3, 2026, President Trump signed into law a funding package to end the brief U.S. Government shutdown.

catalysts.event: President Trump called for increasing FY27 U.S. military budget to $1.5 trillion
catalysts.sentence: on January 7, 2026, President Trump called for increasing the FY27 U.S. military budget to $1.5 trillion...

tamClaims.claim: Boeing projects demand for 43,600 new airplanes over the next 20 years.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on long-term global economic growth projections of 2.3% in average annual gross domestic product (GDP) growth, Boeing projects demand for 43,600 new airplanes over the next 20 years.

tamClaims.claim: Airbus projects demand for 43,420 passenger plus freighter aircraft over the next 20 years.
tamClaims.sentence: Airbus demand projections are similar at 43,420 passenger plus freighter aircraft over the next 20 years.

tamClaims.claim: IATA estimates industry-wide profits of $39.5 billion for 2025, forecasting $41 billion for 2026.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the International Air Transport Association (IATA), it is estimating industry-wide profits of $39.5 billion for 2025, an increase from $28.3 billion in 2024. For 2026, IATA is forecasting $41 billion in profits for the industry globally.

tamClaims.claim: Airbus Global Services Forecast for Asia-Pacific anticipates services demand growth at 5.2% CAGR through 2044, reaching $138.7 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: its Global Services Forecast for Asia-Pacific (including China and India) anticipates that total services demand in the region will grow at a 5.2% compound annual growth rate through 2044, reaching an estimated market value of $138.7 billion.
